rulings.Case Title: EUGENE CATANIA ET AL VS. MAURIZIO IANTORNO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Entry Of Default Per Ccp Section 473 And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 29, 2024 line 1. DEFENDANT MAURIZIO IANTORNO , AN INDIVIDUAL, 261 SILVER, LLC, A PURPORTED CALIFORNIA LIMITED's MOTION TO SET ASIDE ENTRY OF DEFAULT PER CCP SECTION 473 AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS. OFF CALENDAR per the request of moving party. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: EQUITY RESIDENTIAL MANAGEMENT, LLC VS. MOTOYOSHI KOMORI ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 29, 2024 line 3. DEFENDANT MOTOYOSHI KOMORI's MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. GRANTED. Under CCP 1166(a)(1), a complaint for unlawful detainer must be verified and include the printed name of the person verifying the complaint. Plaintiff's First Amended Complaint indicates in the verification section of the judicial council form to "See attached" but only includes two exhibits, the lease and the notice. There is no other exhibit containing a verification and the name of the person verifying the First Amended Complaint. Thus, Defendant's Motion to Strike the First Amended Complaint is GRANTED with leave for Plaintiff to amend. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: FRE 398 LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY VS. JESSICA M. DERRICO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiffs Form Interrogatories Propounded Against Defendant Jessica M. Derrico And For Monetary Sanctions Against Jessica M. Derrico
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 29, 2024 line 6. 1-PLAINTIFF FRE 398 LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY's MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO PLAINTIFFS FORM INTERROGATORIES PROPOUNDED AGAINST DEFENDANT JESSICA M. DERRICO AND FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS AGAINST JESSICA M. DERRICO. GRANTED subject to Opposition. Jessica M. Derrico must provide responses to Plaintiff's form interrogatories (set one) and demands for inspection (set one), without objections, no later than 5 days of the Order. Plaintiff's request for $325 monetary sanctions against Jessica M. Derrico is GRANTED. (C.C.P. 2023.010, 2023.030, 2033.280(c).) Jessica M. Derrico must pay counsel for FRE 398 LLC no later than 5 days of the Notice of entry of the Order. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
